titleStmt.title: SII 25.45: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 45. (A.R. No. 45 of 1908.) MĒṬṬUPAṬṬI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the beds in the Pañchapāṇḍavar-paḍukkai in a cavern on the hill.
There are three fragmentary inscriptions which may palaeographically be assigned to 2nd century B.C.

The first inscription reads chaiy-aḷaṉ vintai-ūr kāvi-y meaning the cavern of Chaiyaḷaṉ (i.e., one form Ceylon) of Vintaiyūr. The second inscription reads nākapērūr = atai chāttaṉ makaṉ meaning the son of Chāttaṉ Atai of Nākapērūr.
The third inscription reads ••••• ti ••••• kkāttāṉ ma ••••• ṉam •• ly ••••• This is a fragmentary and badly managed inscription.
Besides the above, there are nine other fragmentary inscriptions here. They can be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 2nd century B.C.
The first inscription reads: antai ariyti meaning a personal name Antai Ariyti.
The second inscription reads: antai iravātaṉ meaning a personal name Antai Iravātaṉ.
The third inscription reads: matira antai visuvaṉ meaning Antai Visuvaṉ of Matirai.
The fourth record reads: antai chenta-a-tāṉa meaning the gift of Antai Chēntaṉ.
The fifth inscription reads: chantaitai chantaṉ meaning a personal name Chantantai Chantaṉ.
The sixth record reads: patiṉ-ūr atai meaning a person A[n*]tai of Patiṉūr.
The seventh inscription reads: kuvira antai chey-a tāṉa meaning the gift made by Kuvira Antai.
The eighth inscription reads: kuvira antai vēḷ-a tāṉa meaning the gift of Kuvira Antai, a vēḷ
The ninth record reads: tiṭi-il-a-tāṉa meaning the gift of (the people of) Tiṭi-il.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p: Digital edition of SII 25.45 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
